notes: Canonical official online repository of German federal laws. Published by the Federal Ministry of Justice with Juris. Used as the SecID namespace for German national laws including EU directive transpositions.

data.description: German Payment Services Supervision Act transposing PSD2. Includes Strong Customer Authentication (SCA), open banking (AISP/PISP), and operational/security requirements for payment service providers.
data.notes: Enacted 17 July 2017, effective 13 January 2018. Supervised by BaFin. Transposes Directive (EU) 2015/2366 — see secid:regulation/europa.eu/psd2.
Adoption process extended into 2025. Transposes Directive (EU) 2022/2555 — see secid:regulation/europa.eu/nis2.

data.description: German Federal Data Protection Act supplementing GDPR with German-specific provisions.

match_nodes.description: SGB V — Sozialgesetzbuch (Fünftes Buch) / Social Code Book V (statutory health insurance)

data.description: German Social Code Book V governing statutory health insurance (Gesetzliche Krankenversicherung).
data.notes: German statutory health-insurance code. §393 (in force 2024-07-01) governs cloud use in healthcare and mandates a BSI C5 attestation.

children.description: §393 SGB V — Cloud-Einsatz im Gesundheitswesen; statutory mandate for a BSI C5 attestation

data.official_name: § 393 SGB V — Cloud-Einsatz im Gesundheitswesen; Verordnungsermächtigung
data.description: Permits processing of health data via cloud services only where the provider holds a current BSI C5 attestation (C5-Typ1 until 2025-06-30, C5-Typ2 from 2025-07-01; a comparable or higher-security standard may substitute, as determined by the BMG together with the BSI). In force 2024-07-01.
data.notes: Statutory mandate for BSI C5 in the German healthcare / statutory-health-insurance sector — this is the law that *references* C5, whereas C5 itself is the control catalogue. Cross-reference: the mandated control catalogue is secid:control/bsi.bund.de/c5 (AI companion: secid:control/bsi.bund.de/ai-c4). This law→control link will be formalized in the Relationships layer (SPEC §6); documented here in notes for now.
